trim_reasons: Blowout risk at -10.5 spread is real: even after the blowout penalty was applied, Doncic as the primary star faces genuine Q4 rest risk when LAL leads big, and the FG_MARGIN_THIN flag at exactly the 10% boundary means one cold-shooting night shaves him below 25. The structural floor is contingent on minutes/game-script cooperation, not guaranteed.

Reaves' quant best_tier is T1 yet the pick is placed at T2, meaning the floor is at a tier the quant data does not confirm as qualifying — the T2 label is doing work the data doesn't support. In a blowout where LAL empties the bench in Q4, Reaves' shot volume compresses precisely when he needs a second make to clear T2.

B2B with b2b_hit_rate listed as insufficient (<5g), meaning there is no reliable B2B baseline — the 89% overall rate may not translate today. Harden's min_floor of 29 vs avg 35 represents a meaningful 6-minute gap, and a load-management-lite B2B rest scenario could easily push minutes toward that floor, threatening a T15 prop that requires sustained volume.

B2B with no usable b2b_hit_rate data is the core issue: Harden's AST production historically requires sustained half-court creation sequences, and compressed B2B minutes (floor=29) directly reduce his opportunity count. The prior audit flagged Harden AST as volatile in contested games; this is a close spread (-2.5) meaning MIA defense stays engaged all four quarters.

VOLATILE tag combined with B2B creates a structurally wide distribution: even though b2b_hit_rate_T15=100% over 10 games is strong, prior audit history shows Mitchell's PTS floor is fragile against engaged defenses in close games, and the typical_miss of 3.5 pts below threshold means a miss lands around 11-12 PTS — well short. Confidence is already at the 70% floor, leaving no margin for the volatility penalty's real-world tail.

B2B at -12.5 spread is the sharpest structural vulnerability on this card: even with a 100% B2B hit rate at T20, a blowout of this magnitude creates genuine Q4 rest risk for Jokic specifically — DEN has strong incentive to sit him early in the fourth when the game is decided. The spread exceeds the implicit blowout threshold and team_momentum is only neutral, meaning garbage-time management is plausible.

SGA's raw_avg_3PM of 1.2 is razor-thin above the T1 threshold, and the VOLATILE tag means his three-point output is genuinely lumpy — the 80% overall hit rate at T1 is respectable but a single cold-shooting night (typical_miss = 1.0 below threshold means he hits zero threes) produces a miss. Against a tough BOS defense that suppresses volume, this is the most fragile prop on SGA's card.

FG_MARGIN_THIN at only 4% combined with VOLATILE tag forced a step down to T15 — but Kawhi's min_floor of 24.8 means a single normal low-minutes game (within his established variance) puts him at ~25 minutes where scoring 15 is not assured. The 4% FG margin means he is one cold-shooting half from a miss, and his volatility means the distribution tail is wide enough that 70% confidence overstates structural reliability at this margin.
